channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tensions escalate as Iran maintains control over the Strait of Hormuz amid global pressure
item.description: Tensions over the Strait of Hormuz continue to escalate as Iran firmly asserts its control over the vital waterway, which has become a key global flashpoint. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian condem...

item.title: Iran and Israel Intensify War Efforts Amid Trump's Ultimatum on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On March 23, 2026, tensions in the Iran-Israel conflict escalated as President Donald Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran, threatening to obliterate the country’s power plants if Tehran did not o...

item.title: IGP Disu Clarifies Order on Reducing Police Tactical Teams, Not Full Disbandment
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, Olatunji Disu, don come out address one matter wey dey circulate online say him don dissolve all police units and squads for the country. According to statement wey th...

item.title: IEA Chief Warns of Global Economic Risks Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: The chief of the International Energy Agency (IEA) has raised serious concerns over the potential global economic consequences of the ongoing crisis in the Strait of Hormuz. This critical maritime cho...

item.title: NASA’s Webb Telescope Discovers Unexpected Atmosphere on Super Earth TOI-561 b
item.description: Astronomers using NASA's James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have uncovered a surprising discovery about the planet TOI-561 b, an ancient and ultra-hot super Earth. Previously thought to be a barren 'la...

item.title: Elderly Woman Passes Away in High-Rise Fire in Goregaon, Mumbai
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Goregaon, Mumbai, where a 72-year-old woman lost her life in a fire at a high-rise residential building. The fire reportedly broke out in the evening, and local authoriti...

item.title: Trump and UK’s Starmer agree on need to reopen Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East conflict
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer held a phone call to discuss the ongoing crisis in the Middle East, with a focus on efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key ma...

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent suggests U.S. may need to intensify attacks on Iran to end the conflict
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated on NBC’s Meet the Press that the United States might need to escalate military actions against Iran in order to ultimately de-escalate and wind down the on...

item.title: Prudence Sekgodiso makes history with gold at World Indoor Championships
item.description: On March 23, 2025, Prudence Sekgodiso became the first South African woman to win a medal at the World Indoor Championships, taking gold in the 800m final in Nanjing, China. She executed a perfect pac...

item.title: Woden Bus Interchange Set to Open in April 2026 Following Utility-Related Delays and Cost Increases
item.description: The new Woden bus interchange in Canberra is on the verge of opening after significant delays caused by undocumented utilities and major water mains beneath Callam Street. ACT Transport Minister Chris...

item.title: Disoriented Sperm Whale Successfully Guided to Deep Waters in Sultan Kudarat
item.description: A disoriented sperm whale was safely moved to deeper waters after being spotted struggling near the shoreline of Poral village in Kalamansig, Sultan Kudarat on March 21, 2026. The operation involved l...

item.title: Sandiganbayan allows amended charges against Francisco Matugas in Surigao del Norte fund case
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has allowed prosecutors to amend the technical malversation charges against former Surigao del Norte governor and now Representative Francisco Matugas, along with former provincial b...

item.title: City of Toronto to Conduct Annual Controlled Burns in High Park and Lambton Park to Support Native Ecosystems
item.description: The City of Toronto has announced that it will carry out its annual series of controlled burns at High Park and Lambton Park as part of a long‑standing ecological management program. These prescribed ...

item.title: UK Government to Hold Emergency Meeting Over Impact of Iran War on Energy and Prices
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is set to chair an emergency Cobra meeting to address the economic fallout of the ongoing war in Iran. The meeting will bring together senior government ministers, including Chancello...

item.title: Fuel Supply in South Africa Remains Stable, No Shortages Expected
item.description: The Fuels Industry Association of South Africa (FIA) has reassured the public that the country's fuel supply remains stable despite fears of shortages. Phila Mzamo, spokesperson for the FIA, emphasize...

item.title: PowerBall Jackpot Won on March 20, 2026, Resets to R57 Million for March 24 Draw
item.description: The South African lottery saw a major development with the PowerBall jackpot of over R108 million won in the Friday, March 20, 2026, draw, after 15 consecutive rollovers without a winner. This victory...

item.title: Sky cruising: Africa’s airlines juggling record‑high and volatile jet fuel prices
item.description: News ya Africa inainua kushangaza kwamba bei ya kerosene ya ndege inabadilika kwa mshahara wa kasi kama kuna mgema kusema, na kuna kuchoka kwa kuomba kwa kero kwa kila kitaratimu ambayo kuna junction ...

item.title: SpaceX’s AI Sat Mini: orbital data centers with 180‑meter solar arrays
item.description: SpaceX is developing a new series of satellites called AI Sat Mini that will function as orbital data centers powered by large solar arrays roughly 180 meters long, each designed to generate around 10...

item.title: Oneida Nation continues to face water challenges despite upcoming pipeline
item.description: The Oneida Nation of the Thames held World Water Day celebrations on Sunday, bringing together around 50 community members to reflect on the importance of water and the ongoing challenges in accessing...

item.title: ACTO Leads Nationwide Transport Holiday as Fuel Prices Surge, Drivers Demand Government Aid
item.description: On March 23, 2026, thousands of public utility vehicle drivers across the Philippines joined a transport holiday organised by the Alliance of Concerned Transport Organizations (ACTO). This protest act...
